paper_title: Applying pollen DNA metabarcoding to the study of plant–pollinator interactions

methods.description: This method involves collecting bee specimens to demonstrate the proof-of-concept of using DNA metabarcoding to construct a pollination network.

steps.description: Sampled bees via aerial netting along 200 × 2-m transects between 10:00 A.m. and 11:00 A.m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bee specimens using aerial netting

steps.description: Collected each bee specimen into its own separate ethyl acetate kill jar to prevent pollen cross-contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: store bee specimens in separate containers

steps.description: Identified pinned bee specimens using Michener (2007) and Discover Life keys.
steps.searchable_summary: identify bee species using taxonomic keys

methods.description: This method describes the process of extracting pollen from bee specimens for further analysis.

steps.description: Extracted pollen by placing bees in microcentrifuge tubes with water and dish soap, then vortexing until no pollen remained.
steps.searchable_summary: remove pollen from bees using vortexing

steps.description: Centrifuged the tube until the supernatant was clear or a pellet formed, then stored the pollen pellet at −20°C.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge pollen suspension and store pellet

methods.description: This method involves isolating DNA from pollen samples using specific kits to prepare for sequencing.

steps.description: Isolated DNA using the Macherey-Nagel NucleoSpin Food kit following the protocol for genomic DNA from honey or pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate DNA from pollen using NucleoSpin Food kit

steps.description: Conducted homogenization in a Mini-BeadBeater-96 for 2 min after adding Buffer CF.
Included 'isolation negative' controls using water instead of pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: use negative controls for DNA isolation validation

methods.description: This method describes the PCR amplification and sequencing of DNA to identify plant species from pollen samples.

steps.description: Conducted PCR using dual-indexing strategy with primers for ITS2 and rbcL gene regions.
Included PCR negative control for each batch and confirmed success using agarose gel electrophoresis.
steps.searchable_summary: validate PCR success with negative controls and gel electrophoresis

steps.description: Purified PCR products using Agencourt AMPure XP magnetic beads.
steps.searchable_summary: purify PCR products with magnetic beads

steps.description: Sequenced pooled PCR products on Illumina MiSeq instrument.

methods.description: This method involves processing sequence data to identify plant species using bioinformatics tools.

steps.description: Joined forward and reverse reads using QIIME version 1.8.0.
Removed low-quality reads with USEARCH version 8.0.1477.
Performed taxonomic classification using RDP Classifier for ITS2 and rbcL markers.
steps.searchable_summary: classify sequences taxonomically with RDP Classifier

steps.description: Used negative controls to set threshold values for sequence removals.
steps.searchable_summary: set sequence removal thresholds using negative controls

methods.description: This method involves constructing and analyzing a pollination network from the identified plant species and bee interactions.

steps.description: Constructed a pollination network by matching bee specimens to plant taxa in their pollen loads.
steps.searchable_summary: build pollination network from bee-plant interactions

steps.description: Plotted the network and calculated bipartite network metrics using the 'bipartite' package in R.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize and analyze network metrics in R

steps.description: Assessed statistical significance of network metrics using permutation tests with 9999 null models.
steps.searchable_summary: validate network metrics with permutation tests
